arts.ttl: Man City XI vs Aston Villa: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for Premier League
arts.sum: Changes expected for a huge clash in the Champions League race

arts.ttl: Aston Villa starting XI vs Man City predicted
arts.sum: Predicted Villa XI: Martinez, Cash, Konsa, Mings, Digne, Kamara, Onana, Rogers, Tielemans, Ramsey, Watkins. Ollie Watkins was largely unplayable against Newcastle and could once again lead the line, even though Villa boss Emery has started Marcus Rashford in five of the club’s last seven matches.

arts.ttl: Man United & Man City eyeing “very good player”, 16-G/A star could be signed for €50m
arts.sum: Manchester United are keen on signing the Monaco winger Maghnes Akliouche at the end of the season. The 23-year-old French winger has done quite well for Monaco this season, and he has six goals and 10 assists to his name in all competitions. He is highly rated across Europe, and Akliouche could develop into a […] The post Man United & Man City eyeing “very good player”, 16-G/A star could be signed for €50m appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Alan Shearer reveals Aston Villa advantage and makes Champions League prediction
arts.sum: Alan Shearer has backed Aston Villa to claim one of the remaining top five places in the Premier League table in the remaining weeks of the season, and in doing so earn another year in the Champions League - and he believes that the Lions have an advantage over some of their rivals. Aston Villa are in a battle with the likes of Newcastle United, Nottingham Forest and Chelsea for a place in the Premier League top five

arts.ttl: Why are Man City letting De Bruyne go? Veteran midfielder still outshining teammates and rivals
arts.sum: Manchester City looked back to their old selves on Saturday, when Pep Guardiola’s side overcame a resolute Everton side 2-0 to continue their push for a top-four finish in the Premier...

Manchester City made it six matches unbeaten across all competitions for the first time since January when they claimed a late 2-0 victory at Everton on Saturday. That victory propelled Pep Guardiola...

arts.ttl: Why the Premier League is now the NFL - and Kevin De Bruyne is the latest victim
arts.sum: We’ve also got an organisation which lionises destructive players, generally has one star dictating the play and has wide receivers - wingers - who get the ball fired into their path to run onto. Why the Premier League is now the NFL - and Kevin De Bruyne is the latest victim

arts.ttl: 'Sooner or later' - Man City players convinced by recent change at club
arts.sum: Manchester City players feel like things have changed in the last few matches and they are on their best run of form since January.

arts.ttl: Davis' red card against Arsenal upheld, Schär escapes sending off in Villa clash
arts.sum: Leif Davis was sent off for a late challenge on Saka, confirmed by VAR. Schär received only a yellow for a foul on Watkins, as VAR deemed it not an obvious goal-scoring denial.

arts.ttl: Villa make it five Premier League wins on the bounce
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Newcastle United - Sat, Apr 19th 2025, 17:30 Aston Villa cruised to a fifth consecutive Premier League victory over the weekend, with an emphatic 4-1 win over Newcastle United at Villa Park on Saturday evening. Ollie Watkins' goal inside the opening 33 seconds gave Unai Emery's men a dream start in B6, though they went in level at half-time after Fabian Schär had restored parity for the Magpies. But a scintillating second-half surge from the Villans saw Ian Maatsen net his first Premier League goal just after the hour mark to put his side back in front, before two quickfire goals - one a Dan Burn own goal forced by Jacob Ramsey, and the other coming courtesy of an unstoppable strike from fellow substitute Amadou Onana - took the game beyond the reach of the visitors. The triumph ensured Villa have now won their last six on home soil in all competitions, and 11 of their last 13 outings overall. View a gallery of images from the clash below...
